Fix chainsaw L/R assignment, INA238 sensor reinit, and mux channel config

- Fix motor 1 INA238 mux channel: ch.5→ch.2 (ch.5 causes immediate bus lockup)
- Fix sensor_reader: retry reinit when _smbus is None at startup (was silently
  stuck at 0.0 forever after failed init)
- Fix reinit methods to acquire _i2c_lock before touching mux (prevents race
  with IMU thread causing [Errno 5] during reinit)
- Reduce reinit-on-null retry interval to 5s so sensor recovers quickly
- Add 150ms I2C bus settle delay before smbus2 INA238 init (after busio BNO055)

commit.message:
v1.5.0: 日勤(7-17時)集客強化 — 8機能追加・5カード統合

新機能:
1. 日勤タイムライン: バス・病院・ホテル・イベント・天気変化を統合表示
2. 次の行動ヒーローカード: 5段階優先度で最適行動をリアルタイム提案
3. 通知強化: 病院退院・ホテルCO・天候変化の15分前プッシュ通知
4. 日勤需要スコア: 複合スコア(履歴30%+病院20%+ホテル15%+交通15%+天気10%+時間帯10%)
5. チェーン提案: 降車地付近の過去乗車地から次の乗車スポットを推薦
6. 戦略シミュレーション: 待機vs流しの時給予測・待ち時間・リスク比較
7. 病院外来スケジュール: 4病院の受付時間・退院ピークをリアルタイム判定
8. 天候予報バー: Open-Meteo API 12時間予報、需要インパクト自動計算

カード統合(5カード削除):
- イベント需要アラート → 日勤タイムラインに統合
- 本日の営業プラン → 日勤タイムラインに統合
- 待機スポット需要指数 → 日勤需要スコアに統合
- 待機スポット売上シミュレーション → 戦略シミュレーションに統合
- 流しエリア需要指数 → 日勤需要スコアに統合

commit.message:
feat: add profile picker and collapse sandbox options in new session dialog (#367)

Add a profile picker field to the new session dialog so users can select
which profile a session belongs to at creation time. Switching profiles
reloads tool, yolo, sandbox, and environment defaults from that profile's
config while preserving user inputs (title, path, group, worktree).

Collapse sandbox sub-options (image, env vars, env values, inherited
settings) into a separate "Sandbox Configuration" overlay accessible via
Ctrl+P on the sandbox checkbox (consistent with path/branch/group pickers).
Enter always submits the form regardless of focused field.

Also adds cross-profile session creation support: when a different profile
is selected, the session is saved to that profile's storage (both for
synchronous and background/poller creation paths).

Fixes #364

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor(gcp_vm_manager, prime_router): enhance endpoint propagation logic and state tracking

- Improved the endpoint propagation mechanism in GCPVMManager to track per-sink propagation states, allowing for retries of only failed notifications to PrimeRouter and UnifiedModelServing.
- Updated logging to provide clearer insights into propagation success and failures, including detailed warnings for partially propagated states.
- Introduced a transition-in-flight flag in PrimeRouter to prevent concurrent promotions/demotions during network I/O, enhancing thread safety.
- Adjusted tests to validate the new propagation logic and ensure consistency in default port values across components.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: homebrew double-nested plugins and stale release artifacts (#68)

The Homebrew formula used Dir["target/bundled/<name>/"] which resolves to
the directory itself. Homebrew's install copies it INTO the target,
creating lib/<name>/<name>/Contents/... instead of lib/<name>/Contents/.
Changed to Dir[".../*"] to install only the contents.

The release workflow cached target/ across builds, leaving old Tauri
bundle output. Globs then picked up stale versioned files (e.g.
WAIL_0.4.10_amd64.deb in a v0.4.13 release). Added rm -rf
target/release/bundle/ before each cargo tauri build.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(uptimeMonitor): two-tier stuck detection + per-service cooldown

Root cause: job_scheduled events with bad data (missing_contact_id) were
triggering false "pipeline down" alerts in the brief window before
processEvents set their process_error field.

Fixes:
1. Two-tier stuck detection:
   - Tier 1: attempts >= 1, no error set (silently stuck in retry loop)
   - Tier 2: attempts = 0, age > 30min (pipeline completely down)
   Both tiers exclude known-error events (process_error IS NOT NULL) and
   exhausted retries (attempts >= MAX_ATTEMPTS), eliminating false positives
   from fresh events that just haven't been touched yet.

2. Per-service cooldown tracking:
   Endpoint failures and Event Pipeline failures now use independent
   60-min cooldown keys so a pipeline blip doesn't suppress endpoint
   alerts and vice-versa.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(http): add /cmd endpoint — REST command proxy for named searches

Implements the bobbin-commands design doc: GET /cmd/{name}?{params}
resolves pinned/default/caller params and dispatches to target endpoint
internally (no HTTP round-trip, no redirect).

Endpoints: GET /cmd (list), POST /cmd (register), DELETE /cmd/{name},
GET /cmd/{name} (invoke). Commands stored in .bobbin/commands.json.

Enables shell alias: alias bob='curl -s "search.svc/cmd/$1?${@:2}" | jq .'

commit.message:
Fix inline redline: pair same-section deletions and additions

P3 (diff engine): Add Pass 3 to match_provisions that pairs unmatched
provisions with identical section numbers. Fully rewritten provisions
now produce MODIFICATION changes instead of separate DELETION+ADDITION,
so _apply_inline_redline renders them inline automatically.

P1 (output generator): Track deletion paragraphs per top-level section
number. When a same-section addition is encountered, append it inline
to the deletion paragraph rather than inserting a new paragraph,
handling any remaining DELETION+ADDITION pairs not caught by P3.

commit.message:
Fix projected mode (vrMode=1): write alpha=1 to forward RT before game's alpha-blend copy

In projected Pipboy mode the game renders to a temporary 1024x1024 RGBA8 forward RT,
then alpha-blends it into srcRT (R11G11B10_FLOAT, no alpha channel). BSLightingShader
outputs alpha=0 by default, so all opaque items were filtered out in that copy — only
BSEffectShader items (Nuka Cherry) with alpha>0 survived.

Fix: after TryRenderShaderGroups renders BSLightingShader to the forward RT, call
DoAlphaWriteToCurrentRTV() to write alpha=1 for pixels where RGB > 0.001. BSLightingShader
items now carry through the game's copy into srcRT and the subsequent composite.

Both wrist mode (vrMode=2) and projected mode (vrMode=1) now display all item types correctly.

commit.message:
Add lane column to team_members and use it for draft context slot labels

Previously the draft-context endpoint used u.primary_role (user's global
profile role) as the lane for each team member. This meant team-specific
position assignments (e.g. SnomJuice playing Top for TTT) were invisible
unless the user had manually set their profile primary role to match.

Changes:
- Migration 1762300000000: adds lane column to team_members with a check
  constraint, and backfills from accepted team_member_invitations so all
  existing members (including TTT) are corrected automatically
- acceptMemberInvitation: now stores requested_lane as lane on INSERT
- listMembersWithPools: uses COALESCE(tm.lane, u.primary_role) so team-
  specific lane takes precedence, falling back to profile role for leads
  who joined without an invitation

After npm run migrate:up, role labels in Pool Snapshot, Player Slots, and
Node Draft Order will reflect each member's actual team position.
